intro: Geophila trichogyne, also known as creeping Jenny or Lysimachia nummularia 'Aurea's relative, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the primrose family (Primulaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Geophila trichogyne include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture for use in traditional herbal medicine, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.
